texto: With deregulation, privatisation, free trade, what we're seeing is yet another enclosure and, if you like, private taking of the commons. One of the things I find very interesting in our current debates is this concept of who creates wealth. That wealth is only created when it's owned privately. What would you call clean water, fresh air, a safe environment? Are they not a form of wealth? And why does it only become wealth when some entity puts a fence around it and declares it private property? Well, you know, that's not wealth creation. That's wealth usurpation.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Elaine Bernard: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “With deregulation, privatisation, free trade, what we're seeing is yet another enclosure and, if …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, ethics, human-rights pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de human-rights e property.
